name: translation_chip_asylum_interview
premise: Asylum officer Daniel Mercer is conducting a final-status interview with an applicant who speaks an endangered dialect for which the agency has no certified human interpreter on site. Headquarters has authorized use of an experimental neural translation earpiece that performs well on everyday speech but struggles with trauma narratives, kinship terms, and indirect expressions of fear. The applicant, Laila Hossam, has traveled for three days to attend this interview and says delaying again could expose her relatives back home because border police have already questioned them about her case. Agency policy allows Daniel to continue if the applicant consents to machine interpretation, but refugee advocates warn that mistranslated nuance can irreversibly damage credibility findings in persecution cases. Daniel can either proceed with the translation chip and let Laila decide whether to accept the risk, or postpone the interview until a human interpreter can be flown in next week.

agents.name: Daniel Mercer
agents.identity: I am an asylum officer trained to build decisions on precise language because a single misread phrase can alter the legal meaning of fear, consent, or political identity. I also know that delay is never neutral when a person's family remains exposed while their case stalls.
agents.goal: I want to preserve the integrity of Laila's testimony without using procedural caution to impose another potentially dangerous delay on her. I need to decide whether respecting her choice to proceed is enough when the translation tool may fail her in ways she cannot detect.

agents.name: Laila Hossam
agents.identity: I am an asylum seeker who has spent months navigating checkpoints, smugglers, and officials who keep telling me to wait a little longer while people back home answer questions about me. I understand the translation device is imperfect, but I also understand the cost of postponement better than anyone in this room.
agents.goal: I want Daniel to let the interview proceed today because I would rather risk an imperfect translation than another week of uncertainty that could endanger my family. I believe I should be allowed to decide which danger is more bearable for my own life.

choices: I will proceed with the interview using the experimental translation chip because Laila understands the risk and the delay may expose her family to further danger.
I will postpone the interview until a certified human interpreter arrives because the stakes of mistranslation are too high to rely on an experimental system.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Daniel Mercer must decide whether to proceed with the asylum interview using the translation chip or postpone it for a human interpreter. What does Daniel Mercer do?
